title: Heterodimensional superlattice with in-plane anomalous Hall effect
type1_summary: Files MOESM2 and MOESM3 are cleaned, labeled source-data spreadsheets for Raman/SHG/XPS and likely transport comparison data, suitable for direct replotting.
type2_summary: File MOESM4 contains condition-specific numeric traces for magneto-transport/Hall measurements with no headers, suggesting underlying measurement data requiring more processing.
classification_notes: The paper explicitly provides source data, but source data are not always raw data. The classification distinguishes cleaned replot-ready spreadsheets (Type 1) from condition-based numeric traces lacking headers and likely requiring preprocessing (Type 2). The PDF is treated as documentation rather than data.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper states that Supplementary Information is provided, including Supplementary Figs. 1–31, Tables 1–4 and References. A PDF in the source-data package is consistent with supplementary documentation rather than a data table.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF format, 3.3 MB, no tabular structure reported. This is consistent with a supplementary information document rather than replot-ready data.
file_classifications.reasoning: This file is best interpreted as supplementary documentation. It is not a dataset file for direct plotting and does not provide structured numerical data in the inspection report.
file_classifications.ambiguity: The PDF could contain embedded figures/tables, but the inspection report does not indicate extractable data tables; therefore it is conservatively classified as documentation.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Supplementary Information PDF; no structured columns reported

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper explicitly states that source data are provided with the paper, and this file is part of the Nature source-data deposit. The reported measurements include Raman, SHG, and XPS, which match the sheet contents.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Excel workbook with three sheets and clear headers. Sheet1 contains Raman shift (cm-1) and intensity columns; Sheet2 contains SHG data; Sheet3 contains XPS data. Small file size and labeled variables indicate figure-ready source data.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is organized, labeled source data that can be directly plotted with minimal processing. It matches figure-related experimental data rather than raw instrument output.
file_classifications.ambiguity: None significant. Although source data are not identical to raw data, the structure is clearly replot-ready.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Sheet1: Raman shift (cm-1), intensity; Sheet2: SHG; Sheet3: XPS

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper provides source data with the paper, and the study includes transport/Hall measurements. This workbook appears to correspond to a figure dataset for transport curves.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Excel workbook with one sheet and 571 rows. The sheet contains labeled series 'VS2' and 'VS2+VS', suggesting processed comparison data suitable for plotting. The structure is compact and figure-like.
file_classifications.reasoning: The workbook appears to contain cleaned, figure-ready data for direct plotting of two series. It is not described as raw instrument output and has the hallmarks of source data.
file_classifications.ambiguity: The sheet name is non-English ('工作表1'), but the data structure and labeled series are still clearly replot-ready.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: 3 columns x 571 rows; labeled series include VS2 and VS2+VS

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper reports transport and magneto-transport measurements, including in-plane anomalous Hall effect under different magnetic-field orientations and temperatures. This workbook likely contains the underlying measured curves for Fig. 4.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Large Excel workbook (914.7 KB) with 29 sheets named by experimental panels and conditions, e.g. PanelA_Bx, PanelA_By, PanelA_Bz, PanelB_IPHE5K, PanelB_IPHE20K, etc. Sheets have no headers and contain numeric pairs only, with values resembling measured field/response traces. The organization is condition-based rather than figure-ready tabulation.
file_classifications.reasoning: Despite being in XLSX format, the absence of headers, the many condition-specific sheets, and the numeric trace-like structure indicate underlying measurement data that likely require interpretation or preprocessing before direct plotting. This is more consistent with raw/unprocessed source traces than cleaned figure tables.
file_classifications.ambiguity: Moderate. Because it is source data from the paper, it could be considered figure source data; however, the lack of headers and the panel/condition trace structure make it closer to raw measurement output than to cleaned replot-ready tables.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: 29 sheets; examples: PanelA_Bx, PanelA_By, PanelA_Bz, PanelB_IPHE5K, PanelB_IPHE20K; 2 numeric columns per sheet, no headers

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports an intrinsically grown heterodimensional superlattice made of alternating two-dimensional VS2 layers and one-dimensional VS chain arrays, synthesized directly by chemical vapour deposition. Structural microscopy identifies an unconventional monoclinic 1T-type stacking, and transport measurements reveal an unusual in-plane anomalous Hall effect persisting up to 380 K, which the authors attribute to Berry curvature effects induced by the one-dimensional VS chains and supported by theory.

figures.description: Growth process and optical images of the VS2–VS superlattice.
Atomic structure of the VS2–VS superlattice, likely including microscopy-based structural identification and lattice/stacking analysis.
Detailed analyses of the VS2–VS superlattice, likely combining spectroscopy and microscopy-derived structural/chemical characterization.
Transport measurements of the VS2–VS superlattice, including the anomalous Hall response with in-plane magnetic field.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports direct experimental measurements from growth and characterization, including optical images, AFM, Raman, XPS, STEM, EELS, transport/Hall measurements, SHG, and MOKE. The text does not specify file formats or raw file names, but these are the underlying collected measurements used to generate the figures.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'Source data are provided with this paper.' It also provides downloadable source data files for Fig. 1, Fig. 3, and Fig. 4, and a Supplementary Information PDF containing Supplementary Figs. 1–31, Tables 1–4 and References.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw measurement data are not explicitly described as a separate repository or archive. The paper only states that source data are provided with the paper, without specifying raw file formats, instrument export formats, or a raw-data repository.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data appear to be provided as figure source data files (XLSX) for selected main figures. The paper also includes supplementary tables (Tables 1–4) in the Supplementary Information PDF, which are likely processed/tabulated results rather than raw instrument outputs.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The paper explicitly provides Source Data files for Fig. 1, Fig. 3, and Fig. 4. Fig. 2 is not listed as having a separate source data file in the text provided.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset semantics are figure-centric rather than repository-centric: the paper offers source data attached to specific figures, plus a supplementary PDF with additional figures and tables. The text does not distinguish raw versus cleaned data in detail, so the safest classification is that the provided downloadable files are processed/source data for figures, while the underlying raw measurements are only implied by the experimental methods. Theoretical calculations are mentioned in the author contributions and abstract, but no separate computational dataset or code is described.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The study uses direct experimental measurements (STEM, EELS, Raman, AFM, XPS, transport, SHG, MOKE), which implies raw measurement acquisition even though the raw files are not separately described.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper explicitly states 'Source data are provided with this paper' and lists 'Source Data Fig. 1', 'Source Data Fig. 3', and 'Source Data Fig. 4' as downloadable XLSX files.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “Source data are provided with this paper.”; “Source Data Fig. 1 (download XLSX)”; “Source Data Fig. 3 (download XLSX)”; “Source Data Fig. 4 (download XLSX)”; “Supplementary Information (download PDF) This file contains Supplementary Figs. 1–31, Tables 1–4 and References.”

inspection_reports.note: PDF file, cannot inspect data content
